paper: Paper 3: Cost-Aware Topology Routing for Multi-Agent LLM Deployments

checks.details: Abstract is approximately 196-200 words (after stripping LaTeX markup). Well under the 250-word limit.
paper.tex line 13: \author{Anonymous}. No real author names anywhere in .tex files.

All citation keys used across .tex section files have matching entries in bibliography.bib. Keys verified: chen_2023, chen_2023_frugalgpt, ong_2024, ong_2025, ding_2024, wang_2024_moa, hong_2023, su_2025, yu_2026, orchestrabench_2026, wu_2023, aggarwal_2024, valk_2025, score_2025, zhang_2025_routerr1, survey_routing_2025, zhang_2025_agentbalance, zhuo_2025, google_2025_mass, agentconductor_2025, sparse_debate_2024, topo_priority_2025, zhang_2023_ecoassistant, gandhi_2024, bats_2025, chen_2024_optima, wang_2025_agentdropout, agentdiet_2025, scaling_agents_2025, agenttaxo_2025, song_2025_dao, masrouter_2025, anymac_2025, zhang_2026, vsakota_2024, smit_2024, cemri_2025, kim_2024, aflow_2025. Zero unresolved keys.
Zero http/https URLs, github.com, gitlab.com, or huggingface.co references found in any .md or .tex section file.

Scanned all 7 section .md files for 20 common AI-generated text patterns (delve, leverage, utilize, it is worth noting, comprehensive, robust, cutting-edge, groundbreaking, innovative, aforementioned, paradigm shift, noteworthy, landscape, multifaceted, pivotal, underscore, realm, facilitate, it's important to note, in this context). Zero matches found.
paper.pdf exists at /Users/rj/eb-paper-claude/paper/output/paper.pdf (101,148 bytes). Non-empty, consistent with a multi-page compiled paper.

CRITICAL: Three conflicting backbone claims. experiments.tex line 65 says 'Claude Opus'. Table 1 caption (results.tex line 9) says 'GPT-4o backbone'. discussion.tex line 21 says 'GPT-4o'. introduction.tex line 1 mentions both. Reader cannot determine which backbone produced the reported results.

methods.tex line 63 contains a raw [FIGURE: ...] placeholder that renders as literal text in the compiled PDF. The CostRouter architecture diagram is not a proper LaTeX \includegraphics or figure environment.
3 broken \ref{} commands from prior audit: \ref{sec:discussion}, \ref{sec:limitations}, \ref{sec:results}. These render as '??' in the compiled PDF.
Section 4.3 defines 6 baselines (always-flat, always-hierarchical, always-debate, FrugalGPT-adapted, BAMAS-adapted, oracle). Table 1 reports only 5 rows, omitting FrugalGPT-adapted and BAMAS-adapted.

Pareto knee inconsistency: Section 3.4 says knee at tau=0.85, Section 5.4 says sweet spot at 0.5-0.7 with operating point tau=0.6. Section 3.1 references 'Section 5.1' for R^2 validation but Section 5.1 is 'Main Comparison' with no cost algebra validation subsection.

blocking_issues.issue: Backbone model inconsistency (Claude Opus vs GPT-4o)
blocking_issues.fix: Pick one backbone name and apply consistently across all sections. If experiments used Claude Opus, update Table 1 caption and Discussion.

blocking_issues.issue: Architecture diagram is a raw text placeholder, not a figure
blocking_issues.fix: Create a proper figure (PDF/PNG) of CostRouter architecture and include via \includegraphics in a figure environment.

blocking_issues.issue: FrugalGPT-adapted and BAMAS-adapted baselines missing from Table 1
blocking_issues.fix: Add both baseline rows to Table 1, or remove them from Section 4.3 if results are unavailable.

blocking_issues.issue: 3 broken cross-references render as '??' in PDF
blocking_issues.fix: Add missing \label{} commands to discussion.tex (sec:discussion, sec:limitations) and results.tex (sec:results).

blocking_issues.issue: Pareto knee inconsistency (tau=0.85 in methods vs 0.5-0.7 in results)
blocking_issues.fix: Reconcile the two claims. Either the knee is at 0.85 (making tau=0.6 an aggressive below-knee choice) or the sweet spot is 0.5-0.7 (in which case Section 3.4 needs revision).

warnings.issue: Algorithm 1 uses verbatim block instead of algorithmic environment
warnings.details: methods.tex uses \begin{verbatim} for Algorithm 1. Top venues expect algorithm2e or algorithmicx formatting.

warnings.issue: Some citations feel tangential or forced
warnings.details: \cite{wu_2023} after 'CostRouter threads the needle' (AutoGen is not about cost threading). \cite{chen_2023_frugalgpt} in conclusion feels appended rather than organic.
Methods Section 3.1 references 'Section 5.1' for R^2 validation of cost algebra, but Section 5.1 ('Main Comparison') does not contain a dedicated cost algebra validation.

summary: Paper 3 is NOT submission-ready. Aggregate review score 6.08 falls below the 7.0 threshold. The core contribution (topology routing as orthogonal cost axis to model routing) is genuinely novel and well-motivated. However, 5 blocking issues prevent submission: (1) backbone model inconsistency between Claude Opus and GPT-4o across sections, (2) architecture diagram is a raw text placeholder, (3) two defined baselines missing from the main results table, (4) 3 broken cross-references, (5) contradictory Pareto knee claims. The 6 standard submission checks (abstract word count, anonymization, citations, URLs, AI patterns, PDF compilation) all PASS. Fix the 5 blocking issues and consider expanding the evaluation (more tasks, second backbone, confidence intervals) before resubmission.
